method: 47 CFR §73.184 Figures M3 — AM groundwave attenuation curves

axes.p_grid: Norton numerical distance p = π·d·f / (σ·λ²) — dimensionless. Subsumes ground conductivity σ, distance d, and frequency f, so a single A(p) curve is sufficient for the FCC band.
axes.value: Sommerfeld attenuation factor A (unitless), multiplies the unattenuated reference field 100·sqrt(P_kW) mV/m at 1 km.

scaling.computation: Compute p = (π·d·f) / (σ·λ²) with d in m, f in Hz, σ in S/m, λ in m.
scaling.field: E(d) = (E_ref / d_km) · A(p) where E_ref = 100·sqrt(ERP_kW) mV/m, evaluated at d=1 km.

provenance: Sommerfeld–Norton attenuation factor A(p) per Norton (1941) and §73.184. Numeric values at the listed p evaluated using A(p) ≈ (2 + 0.3p) / (2 + p + 0.6p²), the FCC's first-order approximation. fcc_sigma_grid_mS_m enumerates the conductivities the FCC publishes pre-tabulated curves for; the engine handles all conductivities via the dimensionless p. Replace with FCC tabulated A(d, f, σ) for filing-grade AM exhibits — the schema does not change.
